title: Superglobal $_GET
description: Access data sent via URL parameters.

slides.title: $_GET – URL Parameters
slides.content:
# The $_GET Superglobal

`$_GET` is an associative array containing variables passed to the script via URL query parameters.

Example URL: `http://example.com/page.php?name=Alice&age=25`

```php
<?php
echo $_GET['name']; // Alice
echo $_GET['age'];  // 25
?>
```

Always check if the key exists before using it to avoid warnings:
```php
if (isset($_GET['name'])) {
    echo $_GET['name'];
}
```

slides.title: Safe Access with isset

quizData.question: Given the URL `profile.php?id=42`, how do you get the id?
quizData.explanation: Data in URL is accessed via $_GET.
